properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 26: Pre-CGT Property with Major Improvements**

Property acquired before CGT commencement (20 Sep 1985). Major improvements in 2010 exceeded 50% of property value, triggering deemed post-CGT acquisition.

**Timeline:**
- 15 Mar 1982: Purchase (pre-CGT)
- 15 Mar 1982 to 20 Aug 2024: Main residence (42.43 years)
- 10 May 2010: Major improvements ($180,000) exceed 50% of value ($320,000)

**Key Rules:**
- Pre-CGT property normally exempt from CGT
- **EXCEPTION**: Major improvements exceeding 50% of value trigger deemed acquisition
- Deemed acquisition date: 10 May 2010
- Cost base becomes: Market value at improvement date ($320,000) + improvements ($180,000) = $500,000
- Capital gain calculated from deemed acquisition date
- Property was main residence throughout: **NO CGT PAYABLE**

**Expected Result:**
- NO CGT PAYABLE - Main residence exemption applies despite major improvements rule

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 108-70 ITAA 1997 (Major Improvements to Pre-CGT Property)
- Section 118-110 ITAA 1997 (Main Residence Exemption)
- Improvements > 50% of value → deemed post-CGT acquisition
- But main residence exemption still applies if used as main residence

user_query: Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 26: Pre-CGT Property with Major Improvements - Property at 223 River Road, Bulimba QLD 4171, pre-CGT with major improvements exceeding 50% of value

additional_info.expected_outcome: NO CGT PAYABLE - Main residence exemption applies despite major improvements rule
